title: Richly Caramelized Korean Pork Wrap
description: A charred and crunchy Korean dish featuring caramelized pork wrapped in a ti leaf, nostalgic of Gangwon cuisine. This nut-free-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together brown sugar, soy sauce, garlic, and ginger to make the caramel sauce.
Add the sliced pork to the bowl and toss to coat with the caramel sauce.
Let the pork marinate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to absorb.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the pork from the marinade and cook in the skillet for 5-7 minutes per side, or until charred and caramelized.
Transfer the pork to a plate and let it rest for a few minutes.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil to the skillet and deglaze with a little water to scrape up the browned bits.
To assemble the wraps, lay a ti leaf flat on a work surface and arrange a few slices of the caramelized pork in the center of the leaf.
Sprinkle some chopped green onions and sesame seeds over the pork.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Fold the ti leaf over the filling to form a wrap and serve immediately.
